title: Onondaga County budget proposal has lower taxes, money for law enforcement, child welfare
summary: Onondaga County Executive Ryan McMahon proposed a 2026 budget with an 11% property tax cut for homeowners, representing a 42% total levy reduction since 2018. The $1.6B budget prioritizes law enforcement ($6M helicopter, $700K drones), lead abatement ($1.2M), early childhood education, and a convention/innovation district around the Oncenter, with revenue increasingly dependent on sales taxes (projected 1.5% growth) rather than property taxes.

political_signal.description: County executive demonstrating fiscal responsibility through tax reduction while maintaining public safety investment and economic development focus; aligns with broader regional innovation agenda through convention/innovation district initiative.

processing_notes: Article is about Onondaga County (not City of Syracuse directly), but references innovation district development relevant to CIO mandate
Micron project mentioned as major revenue driver and construction milestone expected 2026 - potential innovation/economic development opportunity for City coordination
Drone 911 response initiative ($700K) represents emerging technology deployment in emergency services
Convention/innovation district around Oncenter warrants CIO attention for potential digital infrastructure, smart city, or innovation ecosystem development
